sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Game over when trying UP from (37,31) to (31,31) - black block blocks that path. Need alternative route. Player at (43,43) after reset. Goal at (43,25). Red hazards at (25,25), (25,31), (37,13). Black blocks block (31,31).
Plan: Try path UP, UP, LEFT, UP, UP, LEFT, LEFT, DOWN to reach goal via top row 19, then down through red block (25,25) from above.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player at (43, 43) after reset
# Try: UP, UP, LEFT, UP, UP, LEFT, LEFT, DOWN, DOWN, LEFT, DOWN, DOWN, RIGHT

# First, let's try UP, UP, LEFT, UP to see if we can reach row 19
actions = ['UP', 'UP', 'LEFT', 'UP', 'UP', 'LEFT', 'LEFT']

for a in actions:
    action([a])
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    player_pos = None
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['color'] == 'b':
            cr = sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
            cc = sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
            player_pos = (cr, cc)
            break
    go = last_action_result.get('game_over', False)
    done = last_action_result.get('done', False)
    lc = last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)
    print(f"{a}: player={player_pos}, go={go}, done={done}, lc={lc}")
    if go or done or lc:
        break
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player at (43, 43) after reset
# Try: UP, UP, LEFT, UP, UP, LEFT, LEFT, DOWN, DOWN, LEFT, DOWN, DOWN, RIGHT

# First, let's try UP, UP, LEFT, UP to see if we can reach row 19
actions = ['UP', 'UP', 'LEFT', 'UP', 'UP', 'LEFT', 'LEFT']

for a in actions:
    action([a])
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    player_pos = None
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['color'] == 'b':
            cr = sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
            cc = sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
            player_pos = (cr, cc)
            break
    go = last_action_result.get('game_over', False)
    done = last_action_result.get('done', False)
    lc = last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)
    print(f"{a}: player={player_pos}, go={go}, done={done}, lc={lc}")
    if go or done or lc:
        break
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
